name: Support / Resistance Reaction
summary: Trade clean break, reclaim, or rejection behavior around well-defined support and resistance zones.
marketConditions: Clear higher-timeframe levels are in play.
The level is fresh or has obvious liquidity around it.
Price approaches the level with controlled rather than random structure.
sessionFocus: New York retests of session extremes

context: Mark daily and weekly highs, lows, prior session levels, and untouched reaction zones.
Prefer levels with liquidity or repeated reactions, not random midrange points.
Know whether you want breakout continuation or rejection before price touches the level.
setup: Price approaches a defined support or resistance with clean swings.
Sweep or rejection occurs at the level.
Structure around the level compresses enough to define risk.
trigger: Enter on reclaim of support or failure below resistance for rejection trades.
Enter on close and hold beyond the level for breakout trades.
Execute only when the level reaction is obvious and risk is defined.
confirmation: Multiple touches or volume make the level meaningful.
Wick rejection or closing behavior confirms the response at the level.
Next path of price is open enough for planned reward.
invalidation: Price accepts through the level and holds.
Level breaks with range expansion against the thesis.
Reaction stays noisy and whipsaws both sides of the zone.
noTrade: Level is already overused and reactions are decaying.
Another stronger level sits too close behind entry.
Approach is messy with no clean risk boundary.

stopLoss: Place stop beyond the far side of the zone or sweep extreme.
Use zone width to size the trade instead of guessing.
If the zone is too wide, skip the trade.
takeProfit: Target the next clear level or liquidity pool.
Pay partials at 1R if the reaction trade is countertrend.
Hold runners only if breakout acceptance or clean rejection continuation develops.
management: Move stop behind the defended zone after first move away.
Scale out as price reaches the next level cluster.
Exit if price drifts back into the zone and stalls.
timeExit: Close if price cannot leave the zone quickly.
Flatten before session close if the trade depends on active liquidity.
Do not carry a level reaction after the level has already been consumed.
earlyExit: Repeated tests erode the reaction edge after entry.

sizing: Risk from the full zone width, not from a random line in the middle.
Reduce size when trading countertrend level reactions.
Do not add to a trade while price is still inside the zone.
exposureLimits: Do not hold both breakout and rejection ideas at the same time on one level.
Stop once repeated tests show the level is weakening.

journaling: Record the exact level or zone traded.
Note whether the entry was a reclaim, rejection, or breakout hold.
Review whether the zone was fresh, tested, or exhausted.
assistantPrompts: Which levels matter to you most: daily, weekly, session, or intraday swing levels?
Do you trade rejections, breakouts, or both around the same zone?
How do you decide a level is still fresh enough to react?
